title: Fabric Upgrade Compliance
spl:
index=inventory sourcetype="network:inventory" role IN ("leaf","spine")
| lookup fabric_target_version.csv platform OUTPUT target_version
| where os_version!=target_version
| stats count by site, os_version, target_version
| sort -count
description: Tracks switch OS / ACI firmware versions against approved upgrade wave — identifies stragglers and unsupported trains.
value: Tracks switch OS / ACI firmware versions against approved upgrade wave — identifies stragglers and unsupported trains.
implementation: Refresh inventory daily. Drive remediation campaigns for nodes not on target.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Inventory scripted input, SNMP.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `network:inventory`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Refresh inventory daily. Drive remediation campaigns for nodes not on target.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=inventory sourcetype="network:inventory" role IN ("leaf","spine")
| lookup fabric_target_version.csv platform OUTPUT target_version
| where os_version!=target_version
| stats count by site, os_version, target_version
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Fabric Upgrade Compliance** — Tracks switch OS / ACI firmware versions against approved upgrade wave — identifies stragglers and unsupported trains.

Documented **Data sources**: `network:inventory`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Inventory scripted input, SNMP. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: inventory; **sourcetype**: network:inventory. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=inventory, sourcetype="network:inventory". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where os_version!=target_version` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by site, os_version, target_version** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (non-compliant nodes), Pie chart (compliance %), Bar chart (by site).
visualization: Table (non-compliant nodes), Pie chart (compliance %), Bar chart (by site).
